title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
snippet: The article reports on a research study published by the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which analyzes the effects of Meta's algorithmic choices on the distribution and visibility of political content on Facebook, noting that the algorithm punishes official politics and favors anti-European extremists.
description: An Open.online article reports on a study authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, analyzing how Meta's Facebook algorithms influence the visibility of political content, potentially penalizing official politics while favoring anti-European extremists.

International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 Conference Program: Communication and Inequalities in Context
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a participant in multiple sessions at the ICA 2026 conference. Specifically, he is mentioned as a participant in the session 'Theorizing Communication Effects in an Era of Preference-Based Information Ecologies' and 'Clicking Politics on an Algorithmic Feed: Political Efficacy and Longitudinal Information. Selection.'. The conference program, released within the last six months, announces his speaker roles.
description: This content mentions Fabio Giglietto as a confirmed participant and speaker in multiple sessions at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference, with session topics aligning with his research areas.
